title: licania subrotundata
intro: Licania subrotundata, also known as the bitter wood, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Licania subrotundata include treating fever, diarrhea, and wounds, as well as providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion by boiling its bark or roots in water to make a herbal tea, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
